message.content:
Read `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`. I need a focused extract of the mapping section only — the section bounded by `tag::mapping-section[]` and `end::mapping-section[]` markers, or equivalent structure.

For each of the following attribute references that appear in RBSCO-CosmologyIntro.adoc, tell me:
- What the attribute resolves to (display text / anchor / meaning)
- Whether that display text matches current Recipe Bottle vocabulary

Attribute references to check:
- `{at_rbm_system}`
- `{at_sentry_container}`
- `{at_bottle_container}` (and `{at_bottle_container_s}`, `{at_bottle_container_p}`)
- `{at_pentacle_container}`
- `{at_operator}` (and `{at_operator_p}`)
- `{at_workstation}`
- `{at_runtime}`
- `{at_build_service}`
- `{st_image}` (and `{st_image_s}`)
- `{st_container_s}`
- `{rbsc_crucible}` (and `{rbsc_crucible_s}`)
- `{rbtgo_ark_inspect}`

Additionally, search the mapping section for any attribute references to legacy terms that may have been renamed: "Bottle Service", "censer", "Mason" (role), "Auditor".

Also search `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/` for any existing references to "Bottle Service" or "censer" in .adoc files — show files and line numbers.

Report compactly: a table of attribute → resolved display text → stale? (yes/no/unclear) → note. Don't propose fixes. Just report facts.

Thoroughness: medium.

content.content:
1	// RBSCO-CosmologyIntro.adoc
2	//
3	// Recipe Bottle Cosmology Overview — the nosebleed-terse "what this is"
4	//
5	// PURPOSE: This document is the index.html frontmatter source for the
6	// Recipe Bottle project page (scaleinv.github.io/recipebottle).
7	//
8	// ANCESTRY: Renamed from index.adoc (last committed 84323c31, 2025-08-18).
9	// Full git lineage: 31 commits from 2642121c through 84323c31.
10	//
11	// DEPENDENCY: Requires RBS0-SpecTop.adoc mapping section for attribute resolution.
12	
13	== Overview
14	
15	The
16	{at_rbm_system}
17	enables developers to safely run untrusted containers -
18	a significantly distinct use case than typical container deployments of carefully crafted code.
19	
20	While containers excel at packaging known applications,
21	running third-party or experimental code poses security risks.
22	The
23	{at_rbm_system}
24	addresses this by interposing a security layer
25	({at_sentry_container})
26	between untrusted containers
27	({at_bottle_container_s})
28	and system resources,
29	without requiring modifications to existing container images.
30	
31	This approach allows developers to leverage the vast ecosystem of containerized tools while maintaining strict security boundaries -
32	essential for small teams experimenting with diverse, potentially risky codebases.
33	
34	== Accomplished Infrastructure
35	
36	The
37	{at_rbm_system}
38	migrated from GitHub Actions and GHCR to Google Cloud Build (GCB) and Google Artifact Registry (GAR) in August 2025.
39	GHCR violated OCI specification for reference counting, and GitHub Actions' shared runner environment enabled cache poisoning attacks — both unacceptable for building containers from untrusted code.
40	
41	GCB and GAR provide isolated build environments, SLSA Build Level 3 attestations (verified end-to-end March 2026), reference-counted layer management, and native vulnerability scanning.
42	The project's build infrastructure was converted from Makefiles to pure bash, and the container runtime switched from podman to docker in December 2025.
43	
44	== The {at_rbm_system} Vision
45	
46	.RBM System Architecture
47	image::rbm-abstract-drawio.svg[RBM Architecture,600,400,align="center"]
48	
49	Maintaining build and service environments is a headache for the small development organization.
50	{st_container_s}
51	are an amazing tool for controlling package version constellations.
52	Necessary, but insufficient; I find that I need more redundancy, security, and control for my build setups.
53	
54	The
55	{at_rbm_system}
56	is my answer for filling in the gaps.
57	The vision is simple: how can an
58	{at_operator}
59	run only a few apps natively on a
60	{at_workstation}
61	to coordinate and arrange
62	{st_container_s}
63	in sophisticated yet safe ways for me and my customer's work?
64	
65	{at_rbm_system} uses only `bash`, `git`, `curl`, `openssh`, `jq` and a
66	{at_runtime}
67	(currently `docker`) natively to produce a safe and controlled space for development involving
68	{st_container_s}
69	heavily.
70	{at_rbm_system}
71	itself is largely a set of bash scripts designed to be easily incorporated into arbitrary projects via
72	`git subtree`, `git subrepo`, or `git submodule`
73	graft.
74	This is purely an open-source undertaking.
75	
76	=== Part One: {st_image} Management
77	
78	The
79	{at_rbm_system}
80	streamlines
81	{st_image}
82	creation and curation through Google Cloud Build (GCB) and Google Artifact Registry (GAR).
83	The
84	{at_build_service}:
85	
86	- Constructs
87	{st_image_s}
88	in clean, isolated environments using **Google-curated Cloud Build builder images**
89	- **Replicates trusted external images** into GAR with digest preservation and provenance tracking
90	- Supports multi-architecture builds via `docker buildx` with binfmt emulation
91	- Generates software bills of material for compliance
92	- Captures and stores full build transcripts for provenance
93	- Maintains build transcripts, SBOMs, and commit references in an **auxiliary metadata artifact** separate from the runtime image
94	- Provides local trust posture inspection ({rbtgo_ark_inspect}) with compact and full views of build provenance, SLSA attestations, SBOM contents, and vouch verification results
95	- Stores validated
96	{st_image_s}
97	in Google Artifact Registry, tagged with minimal non-invasive labels
98	
99	This dual approach - building custom images and importing vetted external ones - ensures all container images used in development have known provenance and consistent tagging.
100	Small teams can maintain a curated registry combining custom-built tools with carefully selected upstream images, all traceable through GAR's unified storage.
101	
102	The system uses only
103	`curl`,
104	`jq`,
105	and
106	`bash`
107	scripts
108	to orchestrate both remote
109	{st_image}
110	construction and registry-to-registry replication, enabling small teams to maintain enterprise-grade
111	{st_image}
112	management practices without complex tooling.
113	`gcloud` is never required on the
114	{at_workstation};
115	it appears only inside Google-managed Cloud Build step containers (e.g. `gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud`) where it runs server-side during remote builds.
116	
117	Auxiliary provenance data, including the per-platform SBOM (`sbom.linux_amd64.spdx.json`), build transcript, build configuration snapshot, and key package summaries, is bundled into a compressed archive and uploaded to GAR as a **Generic Artifact** using the image tag as the package name and a fixed `metadata` version identifier.
118	This design keeps the primary image minimal and broadly usable while making rich build history available to those who need it.
119	
120	=== Part Two: {rbsc_crucible} Orchestration
121	
122	For development services requiring internet and/or IP connectivity at the
123	{at_workstation},
124	{at_rbm_system}
125	then orchestrates startup and configuration of
126	{rbsc_crucible_s}, which are comprised of a
127	{at_sentry_container},
128	a
129	{at_pentacle_container},
130	and a
131	{at_bottle_container}
132	operating together.
133	
134	The {at_rbm_system}
135	allows any
136	{st_image}
137	providing or using network services to function as a
138	{at_bottle_container}.
139	The
140	{at_pentacle_container}
141	establishes a privileged network namespace, configures it to route all traffic through the
142	{at_sentry_container},
143	then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
144	{at_bottle_container}.
145	This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
146	{at_bottle_container}
147	experiences only a functional path to its
148	{at_sentry_container}
149	gateway.
150	
151	The
152	{at_sentry_container}
153	thus sets up a potentially sophisticated set of network security safeguards that prevent malicious or compromised
154	{at_bottle_container_s}
155	from exfiltrating the
156	{at_operator_p}
157	assets.
158	Through configuration of deeply mature tools `iptables` and `dnsmasq`, the
159	{at_sentry_container}
160	prevents such illegal accesses.
161	
162	By providing these controlled yet accessible tools, the
163	{at_rbm_system}
164	enables small development teams to maintain proper container hygiene throughout their workflow - from initial building through deployment and eventual cleanup.
165	This empowers organizations to leverage the expanding ecosystem of containerized development tools without requiring specialized DevOps expertise.
166	
167	== Project Direction
168	
169	The following themes represent future directions for Recipe Bottle, drawn from the project's horizon roadmap.
170	
171	**Build pipeline security hardening.**
172	Egress lockdown, VPC Service Controls, cosign image signing, and Binary Authorization are evaluated and consciously deferred.
173	Each has a defined trigger condition — they activate when compliance requirements, threat models, or deployment targets change.
174	Full analysis lives in the Cloud Build security posture documentation.
175	
176	**IAM identity infrastructure.**
177	Service account numeric IDs for faster propagation, universal read-back verification after IAM grants, and universal SA preflight checks.
178	These reduce retry frequency and strengthen grant correctness guarantees.
179	
180	**Container runtime expansion.**
181	Rootless deployment, macOS-native workflows, and alternative container runtimes beyond the current docker-on-Linux model.
182	
183	== Links
184	
185	Source repository: https://github.com/scaleinv/recipebottle
186	
187	Project page: https://scaleinv.github.io/recipebottle
188	
189	== Significant Events
190	
191	// Deep spelunk helper: $ git log --remotes=origin --all --graph --name-status
192	
193	[%header,cols="1,3"]
194	|===
195	| Date | Event
196	
197	// | Feb 23, 2022
198	// | Got Docker ID.
199	// [cnmp:73066af]
200	
201	| Dec 10, 2023
202	| First try at wrangling Docker in local cygwin environment.
203	// [cnmp:4009f87]
204	
205	| Mar 18, 2024
206	| Embrace multiple ephemeral docker container images for single build.
207	// [cnmp:60f0cf2]
208	
209	| Aug 7, 2024
210	| Started Jupyter Notebook in docker container.
211	// [cnmp:22d2fce]
212	
213	| Aug 11, 2024
214	| Learned docker can't connect to host and internal network simultaneously.
215	// [cnmp:3b20684]
216	
217	| Aug 17, 2024
218	| Switched to podman for better networking.
219	// [cnmp:3a47915]
220	
221	| Sep 2, 2024
222	| First attempt to use asciidoc for concept models.
223	
224	| Oct 7, 2024
225	| GitHub Action performed docker
226	{st_image}
227	build.
228	
229	| Nov 2024
230	| Bespoke
231	{at_bottle_container}
232	images with custom networking use
233	{at_sentry_container}
234	networking via Claude curated requirements and script generation.
235	
236	| Dec 2024
237	| Experiments to start unmodified
238	{at_bottle_container}
239	images using podman networking configurations fail.
240	
241	| Dec 31, 2024
242	| Submitted Podman feature request https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24920[issue #24920] for gateway feature.
243	
244	| Jan 6, 2025
245	| Feature request https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24920[issue #24920] rejected.
246	
247	| Jan 2025
248	| Podman 5.2 VMs running unmodified
249	{at_bottle_container}
250	images using VM network namespace machinations.
251	
252	| Feb 2025
253	| Podman 5.3 VM update breaks previous, and reversion to 5.2 fails due to reissued VM.  Podman VM tags determined not immutable.
254	
255	| Mar 2025
256	| Developed full reproducibility scenarios and determined that VM network namespace approach no longer valid.
257	
258	// | Apr 2025
259	// | Attempted podman-unshare approach to access network namespaces.
260	
261	| June 25, 2025
262	| Attempting to use eBPF frame rewriting to bypass Netavark gateway behaviors lead to third container concept.
263	
264	| July 3, 2025
265	| {at_pentacle_container}
266	as privileged network namespace configurator hosting unprivileged
267	{at_bottle_container}
268	functions; all
269	{at_sentry_container}/{at_bottle_container}
270	test cases pass again.
271	
272	| July 27, 2025
273	| GHCR
274	{st_image}
275	deletions determined unsafe due to lack of OCI-specified reference counting on layers; deleting old images can destroy new ones.
276	
277	| Aug 5, 2025
278	| Abandoned GitHub Actions and GHCR for Google Cloud Build and Google Artifact Registry — isolated build environments, SLSA attestations, reference-counted layer management.
279	
280	| Aug 23, 2025
281	| First GCP project provisioned with role-based service accounts: Governor, Director, Mason, and Retriever — no single credential has full access.
282	
283	| Dec 29, 2025
284	| Switched container runtime from podman to docker. Entire bottle lifecycle rewritten from Makefile to pure bash.
285	
286	| Jan 13, 2026
287	| Job Jockey initiative tracker rewritten in Rust, enabling structured multi-heat development with machine-readable commit discipline.
288	
289	| Mar 2, 2026
290	| Cloud Build v2 migration: replaced Developer Connect with CB v2 connections and Secret Manager PAT retrieval, decoupling the build pipeline from GitHub.
291	
292	| Mar 5, 2026
293	| SLSA Build Level 3 provenance verified end-to-end — single-architecture and multi-platform, dual provenance signatures (v0.1 and v1).
294	
295	| Mar 13, 2026
296	| Bind vessel mode operational: upstream image mirroring to GAR with digest fidelity verification and containerized SBOM generation.
297	
298	| Mar 14, 2026
299	| Graft vessel mode specified: local image push to GAR as third ark arrival path alongside conjure and bind.
300	
301	|===
302

content.content:
// Begin Mapping Section
// tag::mapping-section[]

// Category declarations:
// rbtr_ prefix: RB Term Role (Recipe Bottle role identities)
// rbtgi_ prefix: RB Term Google Instance (Recipe Bottle specific instances)
// rbtgo_ prefix: RB Google Operation (Recipe Bottle workflows)
// gcp_ prefix: Google Cloud Platform (core infrastructure concepts)
// gar_ prefix: Google Artifact Registry (generic service)
// gcs_ prefix: Google Cloud Storage (generic service)
// gcb_ prefix: Google Cloud Build (generic service)
// giam_ prefix: Google Cloud Identity and Access Management
// rbtc_ prefix: RB Term Colophon (tabtarget entry points)
// rbrr_ prefix: Repository Regime configuration

// Local Architecture Terms (bottle deployment architecture):
// at_ prefix: Architectural Term (core system components)
// st_ prefix: Support Term (infrastructure and technical concepts)

// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//   _ed: past tense (verbs)
//   _ing: progressive form (verbs)

// RB Role Infrastructure
:rbtr_role:                  <<rbtr_role,RB Role>>
:rbtr_role_s:                <<rbtr_role,RB Roles>>

// Service Account Hierarchy
:rbtr_consumer:              <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Role>>
:rbtr_consumer_p:            <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Role's>>
:rbtr_consumer_s:            <<rbtr_consumer,Consumer Roles>>
:rbtr_director:              <<rbtr_director,Director Role>>
:rbtr_director_s:            <<rbtr_director,Director Roles>>
:rbtr_governor:              <<rbtr_governor,Governor Role>>
:rbtr_marshal:               <<rbtr_marshal,Marshal Role>>
:rbtr_mason:                 <<rbtr_mason,Mason Role>>
:at_operator:                <<at_operator,Operator>>
:at_operator_p:              <<at_operator,Operator's>>
:at_operator_s:              <<at_operator,Operators>>
:rbtr_payor:                 <<rbtr_payor,Payor Role>>
:rbtr_payor_p:               <<rbtr_payor,Payor Role's>>
:rbtr_retriever:             <<rbtr_retriever,Retriever Role>>
:rbtr_retriever_s:           <<rbtr_retriever,Retriever Roles>>

// Recipe Bottle Term Google Instances
:rbtgi_build_bucket:         <<rbtgi_build_bucket,RB Build Bucket>>
:rbtgi_build_bucket_s:       <<rbtgi_build_bucket,RB Build Buckets>>
:rbtgi_repository:           <<rbtgi_repository,RB Container Repository>>
:rbtgi_repository_s:         <<rbtgi_repository,RB Container Repositories>>
:rbtgi_depot:                <<rbtgi_depot,RB Depot>>
:rbtgi_depot_project:        <<rbtgi_depot_project,RB Depot Project>>
:rbtgi_depot_project_s:      <<rbtgi_depot_project,RB Depot Projects>>
:rbtgi_image:                <<rbtgi_image,RB Image>>
:rbtgi_image_s:              <<rbtgi_image,RB Images>>
:rbtgi_json_key_file:        <<rbtgi_json_key_file,JSON Key File>>
:rbtgi_json_key_file_s:      <<rbtgi_json_key_file,JSON Key Files>>
:rbtgi_metadata:             <<rbtgi_metadata,RB Metadata Artifact>>
:rbtgi_metadata_s:           <<rbtgi_metadata,RB Metadata Artifacts>>
:rbtgi_payor_project:        <<rbtgi_payor_project,RB Payor Project>>
:rbtgi_rbra_file:            <<rbtgi_rbra_file,RBRA File>>
:rbtgi_rbra_file_s:          <<rbtgi_rbra_file,RBRA Files>>
// CB v2 Connection and Repository eliminated (₣Av) — builds.create + pouch replaces triggers
:rbtgi_cbv2_connection:   CB v2 Connection (eliminated)
:rbtgi_cbv2_repository:  CB v2 Repository (eliminated)

// Recipe Bottle Term Google Ark (coherent build output unit)
:rbtga_ark:                  <<rbtga_ark,RB Ark>>
:rbtga_ark_s:                <<rbtga_ark,RB Arks>>
:rbtga_hallmark:             <<rbtga_hallmark,Hallmark>>
:rbtga_ark_image:            <<rbtga_ark_image,Ark Image Artifact>>
:rbtga_ark_about:            <<rbtga_ark_about,Ark About Artifact>>
:rbtga_ark_vouch:            <<rbtga_ark_vouch,Ark Vouch Artifact>>
:rbtga_vessel:               <<rbtga_vessel,Vessel>>
:rbtga_vessel_s:             <<rbtga_vessel,Vessels>>

// Recipe Bottle Foundry Fact Files (output contract identifiers)
:rbf_fact_hallmark:          <<rbf_fact_hallmark,RBF_FACT_HALLMARK>>
:rbf_fact_build_id:          <<rbf_fact_build_id,RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID>>
:rbf_fact_gar_root:          <<rbf_fact_gar_root,RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT>>
:rbf_fact_ark_stem:          <<rbf_fact_ark_stem,RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM>>
:rbf_fact_ark_yield:         <<rbf_fact_ark_yield,RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD>>

// Recipe Bottle Common Fact Maps (dynamic output contract identifiers)
:rbcc_fact_consec_infix:     <<rbcc_fact_consec_infix,RBCC_FACT_CONSEC_INFIX>>
// Recipe Bottle Term Google Build Definitions
:rbtgr_rubric:              <<rbtgr_rubric,RB Rubric>>
:rbtgr_rubric_s:            <<rbtgr_rubric,RB Rubrics>>
:rbtgr_build_json:          <<rbtgr_build_json,cloudbuild.json>>
:rbtgr_provenance:          <<rbtgr_provenance,Rubric Provenance>>

// Recipe Bottle Term Google Operations
:rbtgo_ark_abjure:                <<rbtgo_ark_abjure,rbfl_abjure>>
:rbtgo_hallmark_tally:            <<rbtgo_hallmark_tally,rbfl_tally>>
:rbtgo_ark_ordain:                <<rbtgo_ark_ordain,rbfd_ordain>>
:rbtgo_ark_conjure:               <<rbtgo_ark_conjure,rbfd_build>>
:rbtgo_ark_vouch:                 <<rbtgo_ark_vouch,rbfv_vouch>>
:rbtgo_ark_about:                 <<rbtgo_ark_about,rbfv_about>>
:rbtgo_ark_graft:                 <<rbtgo_ark_graft,rbfd_graft>>
:rbtgo_ark_summon:                <<rbtgo_ark_summon,rbfr_summon>>
:rbtgo_ark_enshrine:             <<rbtgo_ark_enshrine,rbfd_enshrine>>
:rbtgo_ark_inspect:              <<rbtgo_ark_inspect,rbfc_plumb>>
:rbtgo_depot_levy:                <<rbtgo_depot_levy,rbgp_depot_levy>>
:rbtgo_depot_unmake:              <<rbtgo_depot_unmake,rbgp_depot_unmake>>
:rbtgo_depot_list:                <<rbtgo_depot_list,rbgp_depot_list>>
:rbtgo_director_knight:           <<rbtgo_director_knight,rbgg_knight_director>>
:rbtgo_director_vouch:            <<rbtgo_director_vouch,rbfv_batch_vouch>>
:rbtgo_governor_mantle:            <<rbtgo_governor_mantle,rbgp_governor_mantle>>
:rbtgo_image_delete:              <<rbtgo_image_delete,rbfl_jettison>>
:rbtgo_image_retrieve:            <<rbtgo_image_retrieve,rbfr_wrest>>
:rbtgo_payor_establish:           <<rbtgo_payor_establish,rbhp_establish>>
:rbtgo_payor_install:             <<rbtgo_payor_install,rbgp_payor_install>>
:rbtgo_payor_refresh:             <<rbtgo_payor_refresh,rbhp_refresh>>
:rbtgo_retriever_charter:         <<rbtgo_retriever_charter,rbgg_charter_retriever>>
:rbtgo_sa_forfeit:                <<rbtgo_sa_forfeit,rbgg_forfeit_service_account>>
:rbtgo_sa_list:                   <<rbtgo_sa_list,rbgg_list_service_accounts>>
:rbtgo_quota_build:               <<rbtgo_quota_build,rbhp_quota_build>>
:rbtgo_trigger_build:             <<rbtgo_trigger_build,rbfd_build>>
:rbtgo_access_jwt_probe:          <<rbtgo_access_jwt_probe,rbap_jwt_sa_probe>>
:rbtgo_access_oauth_probe:        <<rbtgo_access_oauth_probe,rbap_payor_oauth_probe>>

// Recipe Bottle Term Google Operation Groups
:rbtgog_access:                   <<rbtgog_access,Access Probe Group>>
:rbtgog_ark:                      <<rbtgog_ark,Ark Group>>
:rbtgog_depot:                    <<rbtgog_depot,Depot Group>>
:rbtgog_governor:                 <<rbtgog_governor,Governor Group>>
:rbtgog_image:                    <<rbtgog_image,Image Group>>
:rbtgog_payor:                    <<rbtgog_payor,Payor Group>>
:rbtgog_rubric:                   <<rbtgog_rubric,Build Definition Group>>
:rbtgog_sa:                       <<rbtgog_sa,Service Account Group>>
